title: Xanthous Yeasty Peruvian Sweet Potato Picarones
description: A traditional Peruvian dessert featuring sweet potatoes and dried mint, these picarones are yeasty and bouncy, perfect for midnight cravings. They are gluten-free friendly and have a smoky flavor.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the mashed sweet potatoes, yeast, dried mint, smoked paprika, and anise seeds.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the gluten-free flour and granulated sugar.
Add the flour mixture to the sweet potato mixture and stir until a dough forms.
Beat in the eggs until the dough is smooth and elastic.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it is shiny and slightly sticky.
Cover the dough with plastic wrap and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees.
Once the oil is hot, use a spoon to drop small balls of the dough into the oil, flattening them slightly into disks.
Fry the picarones for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and puffed up.
Remove the picarones from the oil with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Serve the picarones warm, dust with powdered sugar if desired.
